recipes.title: Cake Batter Protein Ice Cream
recipes.desc: A high-protein cake batter flavored ice cream at only 135 calories with 22g protein.
steps: Add 240g (1 cup) unsweetened vanilla almond milk, 170g (1 scoop) vanilla protein powder, 15g (1 tbsp) sugar-free cheesecake pudding mix, and 5g (1 tsp) vanilla extract to a bowl.
Mix until smooth and fully combined.
Pour into the Ninja Creami pint container.
Freeze for 24 hours.
Remove from freezer and run on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Add 15g (1 tbsp) sprinkles to the top and run the Mix-in program.
Re-spin on Lite Ice Cream if needed for a creamier texture.
notes: Swap sprinkles for crushed cookies or chocolate chips for different variations.

recipes.title: PB Salted Caramel Protein Ice Cream
recipes.desc: A high-protein peanut butter salted caramel ice cream with a Barebells protein bar mix-in, yielding 465 calories and 45g protein.
ingredients: 8g (1 tbsp) PB powder (like PB Fit or PB2)
1 Soft Barebells Peanut Caramel Protein Bar (mix-in)
steps: Add 400g (about 1⅔ cups) Fat Free Fairlife Milk, 40–45g (about 3⅓ to 3¾ tbsp) 1:1 sugar substitute, 8g (1 tbsp) PB powder, 2g (½ tsp) salt, and 1g (¼ tsp) xanthan gum to a blender. Blend all ingredients for the base.
Pour the mixture into a Ninja Creami pint container and freeze for at least 16 hours.
Before processing, run the frozen pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds.
Process on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Process again on the Respin setting.
If the texture is still powdery after the respin, re-spin again.
Make a hole down to the bottom of the pint with a butter knife and fill with 1 Soft Barebells Peanut Caramel Protein Bar.
Process on the Mix-in cycle.

recipes.title: Strawberry Protein Ice Cream
recipes.desc: A simple strawberry protein ice cream with just 250 calories and 23g of protein.
ingredients: 340g (11.5 fl oz / 1 bottle) Fairlife Strawberry Milk or Strawberry Protein Shake
steps: Blend 340g (11.5 fl oz / 1 bottle) Fairlife Strawberry Milk, 10–15g sweetener, 1g (¼ tsp) salt, and 1g (¼ tsp) xanthan gum for the base.
Freeze the blended mixture for at least 16 hours in a Ninja Creami pint container.
Before processing, run the frozen pint under hot water for at least 60 seconds.
Spin the pint on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Spin the pint again on the Respin setting.
Respin again if the texture is powdery after the second spin.
notes: Approximately 250 calories, 21g carbs, 8g fat, and 23g protein.
